text: Hi, I'm Shawn Fitzgerald with Davy Tree Commercial Grounds Management. Today we are going to be
discussing proper mulching. As you can see, we're on a very impressive commercial landscape site,
and obviously the owner values his landscape and has spent lots of time and money investing in it.
Proper mulching is very beneficial for a landscape such as this, but one of the most
common mistakes I see is over mulching. Let's take a look at a few examples. When mulch is
piled up against the stem, the trunk, or the flare of the tree, it creates excessive moisture
in here, which in turn creates fungus and disease. It also gives insects place to harbor. We want to
pull the mulch away from the tree so it's not up against the flare, stem, or trunk. We want
to make sure that mulch is not piled up against the trunk, the flare, or the flare. We want to
mimic the wild and put the tree back in a natural setting. If we went for a walk in the forest,
you'd never see mulch or turf grass piled up against the trunk or stem of a tree. As we
properly mulch this tree, you can see the excess mulch that we're removing. We'll have enough from
this one tree to mulch three more trees properly. We want to make sure we keep the mulch away from
the trunk or flare of the stem of the tree and pull it out as far to the drip line as possible.
A properly mulched tree is very
beneficial for your landscape. By removing the excess mulch around the stem, trunk, or flare of
the tree, we've reduced the chance for insect and disease. Also, by creating a nice ring around the
tree, we're eliminating the chance for a string trimmer or mower damage. If possible, we'd like
to bring the mulch ring all the way out to the drip line. This is where your absorption and
feeder roots are for a tree of this size. Obviously, that's not always feasible on all commercial sites.
If you'd like to learn more about mulch, you can visit our website at www.multrimmer.com.
If you'd like to learn more about proper mulching, please click on the link below or contact your
Davie representative for a complete site inspection. My name is Sean with Davie Tree. Thanks for
talking mulch. And remember, proper mulching is very beneficial to your property and your budget.
The Real Pigmas
